items.reason: All 20 decisions (DEC-001 through DEC-020) in docs/decisions/standards-overlap-decisions.md have id, question, options_considered, choice, consequences, and projects_to populated. decisions-needed.md maps each required area to a decision ID, and decisions-pending.md tracks remaining items with owner, target_date, and blocker.

docs/decisions/decisions-pending.md has two entries (PEND-001 and PEND-005) each with owner (Andy), target_date (2026-05-15 and 2026-05-03), blocker, and rationale. Target dates are in the future relative to the 2026-04-27 iteration timeframe.

Each decision's consequences section names concrete artifacts the decision deletes/collapses, e.g. DEC-001 'Removes per-function subject-to-person merge branches from supabase/functions/lti-launch-handler/index.ts...', DEC-007 'Removes duplicate source-ID columns from every public table', DEC-010 'Removes duplicated tenant where tenant_id = ... guard code from PostgREST callers'. Multiple decisions also include 'Why not collapse' justifications.

Each decision in standards-overlap-decisions.md has three options_considered including credible aggressive simplifications (e.g. DEC-001 'Collapse every user, actor, profile, and credential subject into one person', DEC-005 'Use LTI AGS line items and results as the only canonical gradebook model'), permissive alternatives, and the chosen middle. The aggressive options are real architectural choices, not strawmen.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json has projection_policy citing DEC-016, scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py generates the per-spec files, and VERIFY.md runs `python3 scripts/generate_spec_dictionaries.py --check` which the PROGRESS log shows passing with 5 projections, 59 objects, 575 fields. The seed includes canonical_objects with projections to per-spec dictionaries.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json defines canonical_objects (canonical.identity.person, canonical.context.class, etc.) with projections lists. Per-spec dictionaries like dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json reference shared canonical fields. The example shared canonical email field projects to OneRoster person.email, Caliper caliper_actor.email, LTI lti_membership.email, and QTI qti_candidate.email per the seed's canonical.identity.person.email projections.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json defines canonical.identity.person as its own object record with fields (id, display_name, email, source_subject_id, identity_kind), allowed values, and explicit projections to OneRoster person, Caliper caliper_actor, QTI qti_candidate, LTI lti_membership, and lti_launch.subject_id. The unification is data, not prose.

Per-spec dictionaries include shared_enums arrays with shared_enum_id, canonical_values, crosswalk (spec-native -> canonical), and reverse_crosswalk (canonical -> spec-native). Example: dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json shared_enum 'grading_progress' maps OneRoster score_status values and LTI grading_progress values to the same canonical values bidirectionally.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json defines a closed privacy_classes list of 9 values (public, operational, directory, education_record, behavioral, sensitive, credential, privacy_governance, system) all citing DEC-019. All five per-spec dictionaries (oneroster, qti, case, caliper, integration-governance) carry the identical privacy_classes list. No depends_on_contents/depends_on_entity placeholders.

data/data-dictionary.seed.json has a migration_generation block citing DEC-020, listing runtime_object_keys, table_order, and relationship_keys. scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py generates supabase/migrations/0001_oneroster_core_demo.sql from the dictionary's relationships, and VERIFY runs `--check` to gate drift. The migration file is generated with header 'Generated by scripts/generate_supabase_migrations.py'.

Every field, object, enum, and privacy class in data/data-dictionary.seed.json and the per-spec dictionaries carries a decision_id. Privacy classes cite DEC-019, shared enums cite DEC-018, canonical objects/fields cite DEC-001 through DEC-009, and projection metadata cites DEC-016/DEC-020. Spot-checking dictionary/oneroster-core.v1.json shared_enums shows decision_id on every canonical value and crosswalk entry.

docs/spec-fidelity-report.md is generated by scripts/generate_spec_fidelity_report.py with copy-exact seam defects: 0 and extension fields missing rationale: 0. VERIFY.md runs both `--report-only` and `--check`. The report shows per-spec breakdown for caliper-core, case-core, integration-governance-core, oneroster-core, qti-core. Every extension field in the table has a non-missing rationale.

scripts/generate_oneroster_core.py, generate_qti_core.py, generate_case_core.py, generate_caliper_core.py, and generate_integration_governance_core.py each emit Markdown and HTML dictionary pages from the per-spec dictionaries. VERIFY runs all five generators plus build_site_docs.py. site/docs/build-an-edtech-app.html shows hand-written prose linking to dictionary anchors (e.g. oneroster-core-dictionary.html#person.email). check_dictionary_artifacts.py verifies coverage.

docs/decision-map-for-builders.md exists and is referenced from docs/build-an-edtech-app.md with plain-language explanations: 'It explains why the guide uses person, class, enrollment, line_item, result, CASE targets, tenant-scoped user JWTs, and Caliper activity records instead of asking app code to reconcile those standards one by one.' The decision register renders as HTML at site/docs/decisions-standards-overlap-decisions.html with anchors per decision (DEC-001 through DEC-020).

docs/build-an-edtech-app.md walks through all five required steps: (1) creating org_build_school with teacher and ten students via curl POST /rest/v1/organizations and /rest/v1/people, (2) creating class_build_math_6a with enrollments via /rest/v1/classes and /rest/v1/enrollments, (3) posting numeric grade via /rest/v1/line_items and /rest/v1/results, (4) linking assignment to CASE URI via /rest/v1/case_items and PATCH /rest/v1/line_items, (5) reading Caliper-style feed via /rest/v1/class_activity_feed. Every field and enum value links to its dictionary entry.

site/api/site-link-check.json reports status: 'pass' with 39 HTML pages, 330 local links checked, 634 dictionary entries, 20 decisions, 12 API endpoints, errors: 0, and reachable_html_pages: 39 (matching html_pages: 39). All pages are reachable from site/index.html within max_clicks: 3.

The guide is comprehensive and links to dictionary anchors, but Step 0 requires the layperson to create their own Supabase project and generate a service-role key from a dashboard - this is a setup prerequisite outside the docs themselves. More substantively, scripts/bootstrap_build_guide.py is invoked but is referenced from docs/build-an-edtech-app.md as if standalone; the layperson must run a Python script (not just curl/SQL) which the rubric specifies should be 'curl commands, SQL statements, and data shapes'. The bootstrap step is necessary but adds a dependency the rubric describes as 'reads only docs/'.
items.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md Step 0: replace the python3 scripts/bootstrap_build_guide.py invocation with explicit curl/psql commands, or document the bootstrap script's exact behavior inline so a layperson can reproduce it without running platform code. Alternatively, surface the bootstrap as a documented one-line setup the rubric explicitly permits.

items.reason: scripts/evaluate_platform.py contains DEFAULT_TRACED_CAUSES dict mapping every rubric ID to a specific lower-layer artifact and required change. ensure_traced_causes() and normalize_traced_cause() enforce non-empty traced_cause for every non-pass item. normalize_buildability_gaps() ensures each gap has step, gap, and traced_cause fields. The evaluator's instructions explicitly require traced_cause to name 'the lowest-layer artifact that must change and the required change'.

VERIFY.md ends with `python3 scripts/evaluate_platform.py --runs 3 --output site/api/platform-evaluation.json`. The script writes site/api/platform-evaluation.json with done flag, per-item status, traced_cause for non-pass items, and buildability_gaps array. PROGRESS.md entries from iter1, iter2, iter3 (2026-04-27) all show evaluator counts and per-item statuses recorded.

scripts/codex_loop.py defines read_overrides(), overrides_paused() (which checks for PAUSE token outside HTML comments), and format_overrides_for_prompt(). The PAUSE check runs at the top of each iteration before any work begins. The prompt builder injects overrides under 'Human overrides from `docs/loop-overrides.md` (AUTHORITATIVE...)' with explicit precedence over rubric defaults.

scripts/codex_loop.py _run_loop() reads after_eval from VERIFY's evaluator output via load_verify_evaluator_output() (which refuses stale mtime), then 'if after_eval and after_eval.get("done"): return 0'. When Codex stdout contains 'LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE' but evaluator says done=false, it logs 'Codex reported COMPLETE but evaluator says work remains. Continuing.' and continues. Termination is verifier-driven, not narrative-driven.

scripts/codex_loop.py maybe_publish() does run_git fetch origin main, then run_git pull --rebase origin main, then run_git push. On rebase failure it captures conflicting paths via `git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U`, runs `git rebase --abort`, and returns 'DIVERGENCE:' which causes _run_loop to print and `return 1` halting the loop. PROGRESS.md captures the divergence in append_machine_progress.

scripts/evaluate_platform.py pins temperature to 0 via deterministic_temperature_for_model() (with documented exception for claude-opus-4-7 which rejects the parameter). VERIFY.md runs `--runs 3`. build_consensus_report() picks the safer (lowest-pass) verdict per item using STATUS_SAFETY_RANK = {fail:0, partial:1, blocked:2, pass:3}, treats done=false from any run as the consensus done flag, and writes the determinism block with every run's counts/statuses and disagreements. scripts/codex_loop.py enforces minimum 3 runs and refuses fewer.

scripts/codex_loop.py append_machine_progress() reads counts from after_eval (the parsed VERIFY evaluator JSON), not from Codex's last_message. When 'LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE' is in final_text but after_eval reports done=false, it appends 'narrative_verify_mismatch: Codex claimed `LOOP_STATUS: COMPLETE`; VERIFY evaluator reported `done=false` with counts {verify_counts}.' The 2026-04-27 iter1 backfill entry in PROGRESS.md explicitly cites VERIFY's pass=20 partial=2 done=false and supersedes the earlier optimistic claim.

scripts/codex_loop.py maybe_publish() calls staged_eval_matches_verify() which runs `git show :site/api/platform-evaluation.json`, applies comparable_eval (which strips evaluatedAt), and compares per-item status, counts, done, and buildability_gaps via summarize_eval_diff. On mismatch maybe_publish returns 'skipped: staged evaluation does not match VERIFY output' before commit, blocking publish.

buildability_gaps.gap: Step 0 requires running `python3 scripts/bootstrap_build_guide.py` which is platform code, not a documented curl/SQL command. The rubric specifies the layperson should produce 'curl commands, SQL statements, and data shapes' from docs alone.
buildability_gaps.traced_cause: docs/build-an-edtech-app.md Step 0: either inline the bootstrap script's psql/curl operations as documented commands the reader can run directly, or explicitly document that the bootstrap script is part of the platform's published tooling (and the layperson is expected to invoke it as a one-line setup).

projections.note: Structural per-decision projection index generated from `projects_to`; semantic contradictions are graded by the projections_match_reality rubric item.

decisions.title: DEC-001 Person, User, Actor, Profile, and Subject
decisions.choice: The platform has a canonical `person` for known school people and a broader `agent` concept for non-person actors. Credential subjects and Caliper actors resolve to `person` only when policy and identifiers allow it; otherwise they remain linked external agents.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-002 Class, Course, Context, Group, Course Section, and Organization
decisions.choice: The platform separates `course` from `class`. `course` is the catalog/template. `class` is the scheduled teaching section. LTI contexts and Caliper groups can map to a class, course, or other learning context. Common Cartridge organizations are content outlines, not school organizations or scheduled classes.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-003 Roles
decisions.choice: Store source roles exactly, then map them to platform role families: `learner`, `educator`, `administrator`, `guardian`, `mentor`, `staff`, `tool`, `issuer`, and `unknown`.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-004 Enrollment and Membership
decisions.choice: `enrollment` is the canonical roster participation record. `membership` is a contextual service/event view that may be derived from enrollment or captured from an external system.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-005 Results, Scores, Outcomes, and Grade Events
decisions.choice: The platform uses `line_item` as the gradebook target and `result` as the learner's current gradebook state. LTI AGS Score is an incoming write/update message. LTI AGS Result and OneRoster Result map to current result state. QTI outcomes are assessment-runtime variables projected into results only when a delivery or scoring workflow declares that mapping. Caliper GradeEvent is event history, not the gradebook source of truth.

decisions.title: DEC-006 Standards Alignment
decisions.choice: CASE is the canonical standards graph. Other standards store alignment as source metadata and resolve known targets to CASE item identifiers or URIs.

decisions.title: DEC-007 Identifiers
decisions.choice: Internal records have platform IDs. Standard-shaped endpoints expose the standard-native ID as the primary contract when required. Every external ID is stored in a source identifier crosswalk with source system, identifier type, and object relationship.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-008 Time, School Sessions, and Event Timestamps
decisions.choice: Separate calendar periods, event timestamps, availability windows, and validity periods. Store timestamps in UTC with source timezone/offset when provided. Store OneRoster AcademicSession as the school calendar backbone.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-009 Content, Resources, Packages, and Launch Links
decisions.choice: The platform uses a broad `resource` concept for discoverable learning objects, with subtype-specific records for cartridge resources, QTI packages/items/tests, LTI resource links, and Caliper event entities.

decisions.title: DEC-010 Tenant-Owned Data and Shared Reference Data
decisions.choice: Tenant-owned operational records carry tenant boundaries and row-level policy. Public CASE frameworks, public standards metadata, public certification fixtures, and optionally public item banks live in shared reference namespaces. Tenants adopt, pin, override, or privately extend shared reference data through explicit records.

projections.detail: Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.
Referenced artifact contains the projection's primary token.

decisions.title: DEC-011 Privacy Surfaces
decisions.choice: Generated docs may describe every field and privacy class because they are schema documentation, not records. Live PostgREST may expose tenant-owned `public`, `operational`, `directory`, and `education_record` records only through tenant RLS and scope policy. Edge Functions may expose `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, or sensitive operational fields only through caller JWT, purpose, scope, tenant RLS, and audit logging when the read is sensitive. Static `site/api/*.json` mirrors may contain only synthetic demo records from the seeded `.test` tenant and may include `public`, `operational`, and `directory` fields such as demo email; they may not contain real tenant data, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive_pii`, or secret/system values.

decisions.title: DEC-012 Runtime Coverage Per Spec
decisions.choice: OneRoster core is runtime-backed now through local SQLite, hosted Supabase PostgREST, RLS, and live tests. The teaching-app runtime also backs the narrow CASE alignment path needed by gradebook line items (`line_items.case_target_uri`) and the narrow Caliper class-feed path needed by the buildability guide (`caliper_events` plus `class_activity_feed`). QTI and full CASE import/search remain doc-only/generated dictionary projections until runtime slices are built. Caliper, LTI, Security Framework, and Data Privacy have partial runtime backing through authenticated table, view, Edge Function receipt, or governance paths, but not full standard conformance flows. Full runtime for QTI import, CASE search, full Sensor API ingestion/projection, LTI Advantage services, production OAuth, and privacy workflows remains future work with target dates in the decision/pending work queue.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-013 Audit Response Truth
decisions.choice: Audited Edge Functions must use truthful responses when they include an `audit` block. If the function cannot read the written `audit_log` rows back under the caller's JWT and request identifier, it must omit the audit block or return an explicit error; it must not hard-code `logged` counts or field lists.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-014 Static Mirror Policy
decisions.choice: Static mirrors are allowed as documentation fixtures for GitHub Pages and offline inspection. They are not the canonical runtime contract; live Supabase REST and Edge Functions are the runtime contract. Mirrors must be generated from the same synthetic demo seed as the local demo, must carry source metadata, and may only include privacy classes allowed by `DEC-011-privacy-surfaces`.

decisions.title: DEC-015 Service-Role Policy
decisions.choice: Request-scoped runtime code and Edge Functions must pass the caller's `Authorization` bearer token to Supabase so RLS applies. Service-role access is allowed only for allowlisted admin or test fixture operations in `docs/admin-operations.md`, with a named operation ID, caller, why-user-JWT-insufficient reason, guardrails, and recent review date. Tests may use service role to create temporary Auth users, but not to read tenant data or satisfy the assertion under test.

decisions.title: DEC-016 Dictionary Generation Direction
decisions.choice: `data/data-dictionary.seed.json` is upstream. `dictionary/*.v1.json` files are generated projections; hand-editing a per-spec projection is a build error once the generator lands.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-017 Canonical Field Reconciliation
decisions.choice: Every overlapping spec field carries `canonical_field_id` pointing at the shared dictionary. Spec-native names remain for conformance; cross-spec app code uses the canonical field identity.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-018 Global Enum Crosswalks
decisions.choice: Allowed values are global dictionary objects. Each enum field references `shared_enum_id`; the shared enum maps every spec-native value to and from a canonical value.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-019 Closed Privacy Classes
decisions.choice: Closed keys are `public`, `operational`, `directory`, `education_record`, `behavioral`, `sensitive`, `credential`, `privacy_governance`, and `system`. `depends_on_entity` and `depends_on_contents` are invalid.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

decisions.title: DEC-020 Relational Graph Migrations
decisions.choice: Shared dictionary objects own source field, target object, target field, cardinality, ownership, and delete behavior. Supabase migrations are generated from that graph.

projections.detail: Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.
Fragment text appears in the referenced artifact.

advisoryScoreGate.note: Advisory only. The deterministic score gate no longer gates publish; this evaluator does.

temperature.note: Anthropic Messages API does not expose a seed parameter. For models that still expose temperature, the evaluator pins it to 0. claude-opus-4-7 rejects the temperature parameter as deprecated, so the loop relies on N>=3 safer consensus for that model.

seed.note: Anthropic Messages API does not expose a seed parameter. For models that still expose temperature, the evaluator pins it to 0. claude-opus-4-7 rejects the temperature parameter as deprecated, so the loop relies on N>=3 safer consensus for that model.

determinism.consensus_rule: If all runs agree, keep the unanimous verdict. If any per-item status disagrees, select the safer lower-pass status using fail < partial < blocked < pass. If any run reports done=false, the consensus done flag is false.
